title: When I go to look at my subscription page to see VPN sub info, page always crashes before displaying any info.
content: I have had so much trouble w/ FireFox VPN. At this point I'm convinced Google is paying the CEO and crash Mozilla straight into the ground. I paid for annual sub, then like something changed (they couldn't honor one of the services (email relay maybe?)) and then I had VPN for a bit and then all of a sudden it got cancelled. Now I have it turned on after they took a while to get back to me about asking why it was turned off (I guess at least they got back to me eventually), but when I go to check my account info about when my renewal is becuase I'm highly thinking about going over to DuckDuckGo and WaterFox, I click on "Paid subscriptions" options and get a refresh of the page w/ a "failed to load page, contact support" every single time. Then when I follow link to "contact support" I get a community forum option, It's giving BROKE!! I really have loved FireFox for so long but I'm thinking this might be the end. I even tried working for Mozilla, but decided not to due to the Five stage interview process, which clearly is not working out for them. Were they really able to screen the best of the best developers with that type of interview process? I digress...

title: Did Not Connect: Potential Security Issue, Firefox detected a potential security threat and did not continue to google.com because this website requires a secure connection.
content:
Did Not Connect: Potential Security Issue

Firefox detected a potential security threat and did not continue to google.com because this website requires a secure connection.

What can you do about it?

google.com has a security policy called HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS), which means that Firefox can only connect to it securely. You can’t add an exception to visit this site.

The issue is most likely with the website, and there is nothing you can do to resolve it. You can notify the website’s administrator about the problem.
creator: yangsengs

title: Will Firefox have a memory usage limit like it is there in Microsoft Edge
content:
In 2024-25 Microsoft Edge gained the ability to limit the usage of physical RAM that the processes uses. Once that limit is reached tabs are put to sleep or are unloaded. So a user of Microsoft Edge could restrict the memory usage to say 1GB or 2GB or 3GB and so on till the maximum available physical RAM.

Does Firefox have such an ability? Is there some flag or settings in about:config that can be enabled for a similar feature to be used? Does Firefox have ability to put tabs to sleep once SWAP is started to be used? Is there some flag or settings in about:config that can be enabled for a similar feature to be used?

If none of one of the above is not available with Firefox then will Firefox add this feature as part of the browser in the future?

title: extem
content:
Marking support threads as "Solved"

The Original Poster (OP), also called the "Question owner", can select Solves the problem next to an answer, to mark the thread they started as "Solved". Moderators can also mark threads as solved when the Original Poster (OP) clearly indicates the problem is solved (such as "thank you, problem fixed now" or "ok, it looks like it worked" etc.). We will not mark as solved threads which appear to be solved and probably are but where the OP did not make any comment. Multiple Accounts

In certain cases, multiple accounts are acceptable for use on Mozilla Support, for example: 

   Moderators or Reviewers who want an account to view or participate in the Mozilla Support site without special permissions.    SUMO members with a recognized role, such as Moderator, who want anonymity to ask a question, for personal privacy.    Users who are having trouble signing in to their account might create a new one.

It's recommended that you indicate any alternate profiles in your primary user profile.

Multiple accounts cannot be used for trolling, spamming, phishing for private information, gaming the helpful votes in the forum, or for any other behavior that violates our rules and guidelines. See also Consequences of Unacceptable Behavior. bold text
